page: Microsoft Azure D96ls_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Dlsv7 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical Intel Xeon 6973P-C cores, utilizing x86_64 architecture with a high base clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The system is configured with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable. The processor features a large 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated mathematical and machine learning operations.

Benchmark evaluations demonstrate strong multi-core processing capabilities, with a Passmark CPU score of 104,787.21 and a stress-ng multi-core CPU performance score of 95,440.36, both placing in the top tiers. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 7,017.2 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 333.72 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. The server excels in data processing and compression tasks, achieving a top-tier bzip3 multi-threaded decompression speed of 575.95 GB/sec and a static web serving throughput of 142.01 GB/sec. Additionally, the hardware-assisted AMX instructions enable high-speed local LLM prompt processing, reaching 304.72 tokens/sec on a medium 7B parameter model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D96ls_v7 is optimized for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. By constraining the memory allocation to 2.0 GB per vCPU, the instance minimizes memory overhead costs while delivering maximum raw CPU performance. This resource density makes it highly suitable for compute-intensive tasks such as high-throughput web serving, multi-threaded data compression, cryptography, and CPU-based machine learning inference. However, workloads requiring large in-memory databases or local scratch disks may face bottlenecks due to the lack of bundled local storage and the low memory-to-vCPU ratio.
description: Microsoft Azure D96ls_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Dlsv7 family, powered by a 96-vCPU Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features 192.0 GB of RAM (2.0 GB per vCPU), a 480 MB L3 cache, and lacks local storage or GPUs. The server delivers top-tier multi-core performance, highlighted by a Passmark CPU score of 104,787.21 and a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression speed of 575.95 GB/sec. It also excels in memory bandwidth and CPU-based LLM prompt processing via Intel AMX extensions. Given its low memory-to-core ratio, the D96ls_v7 offers a cost-efficient profile for compute-heavy, memory-lean workloads. It is highly suited for high-throughput web serving, multi-threaded data compression, and CPU-bound scientific computing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96ls_v7 is a compute-optimized server with 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for high-throughput workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96ls_v7 is a 96-vCPU compute-optimized server with 192 GB RAM, designed for high-performance multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency, 96-vCPU compute-optimized platform engineered for intensive multi-threaded processing, data compression, and high-throughput web serving.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs operating at a high clock speed of 4.2 GHz.
192.0 GB of system memory, optimized for compute-heavy, low-memory workloads.
Top-tier multi-core performance with a Passmark CPU score of 104,787.21.
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated mathematical and AI inference workloads.
No bundled local storage, requiring external Premium Storage attachment.
Best suited for high-throughput web serving, cryptography, and multi-threaded data compression.
